title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Beat NZ school holiday crowds and save up to 30% on travel with our expert tips and tricks
intro: If you're planning to travel during NZ school holidays, be prepared for peak prices and crowds, with some popular destinations like Queenstown and Rotorua seeing accommodation prices rise by up to 50%. However, with some clever planning, you can beat the crowds and save money. In this article, we'll show you how to make the most of your school holiday travel budget.

sections.heading: Plan Ahead to Save Money
sections.body: Booking your accommodation and activities well in advance is crucial to saving money during school holidays. For example, a family room at the Novotel Queenstown Lakeside can cost around $450 per night during peak season, but booking 3 months in advance can reduce the price to around $350 per night.

sections.heading: Consider Alternative Destinations
list: Instead of Queenstown, try Wanaka (around 1 hour drive)
Instead of Rotorua, try Taupo (around 1 hour drive)
Instead of Auckland, try the Bay of Islands (around 3 hour drive)

sections.heading: Make the Most of Free Activities
sections.body: There are plenty of free activities to enjoy in NZ, such as visiting the beach, going for a hike, or exploring a new town. For example, the hike to the top of Mount Eden in Auckland offers stunning views of the city and is completely free.

cta.text: Try our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator

sections.heading: Book a Campervan for Flexibility
sections.body: Booking a campervan can be a great way to travel during school holidays, as it gives you the flexibility to create your own itinerary and avoid crowded hotels. Prices start from around $150 per day for a 2-berth campervan, and you can book one on our campervans page.

sections.heading: Get Inspiration from Our Destination Guides
